rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and data types.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees; avoid implementation-specific subcomponents in type contracts.
generic_rules: Declare boolean range checks using helper equations with explicit lower and upper limits.
Ensure AGREE expression types match AADL data types to prevent type mismatch errors.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or resource constraints directly in AGREE annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible features and variables in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-level subcomponent ports in component type contracts.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean domain values into integer scalars unless explicitly supported by the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints
Model fusion and component implementation binding details
